req.title: Manager views employee
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to view a employee through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: This test suite validates that authenticated managers can view employees via the UI/API with proper validation, audit logging, and error handling. The suite covers the happy path (positive), authorization/validation failures (negative), and edge cases around mandatory field validation (boundary). Tests are designed to be deterministic, idempotent, and traceable to the requirement's stated outcomes and error pathways. Test[3] corrected from BVA to NEG heuristic to align with negative kind classification per ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4.

tests.name: test_manager_views_employee_success
preconditions: Target employee record exists with id=EMP-001
actions: Query audit log for employee view event
expected: HTTP 200 response
Response body contains employee_id, name, department, email
Audit record created with action=VIEW_EMPLOYEE, user_id=manager_id, timestamp present
Audit record status=SUCCESS
tests.executable:
import requests
import json
from datetime import datetime

def test_manager_views_employee_success():
    session = requests.Session()
    auth_resp = session.post(
        'http://localhost:8080/auth/login',
        json={'username': 'manager_user', 'password': '<pwd_fixture>'}
    )
    assert auth_resp.status_code == 200
    token = auth_resp.json()['token']
    
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}'}
    emp_resp = session.get(
        'http://localhost:8080/employees/EMP-001',
        headers=headers
    )
    assert emp_resp.status_code == 200
    emp_data = emp_resp.json()
    assert emp_data['employee_id'] == 'EMP-001'
    assert 'name' in emp_data and emp_data['name']
    assert 'email' in emp_data
    
    audit_resp = session.get(
        'http://localhost:8080/audit?action=VIEW_EMPLOYEE&entity_id=EMP-001',
        headers=headers
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    audit_log = audit_resp.json()['records']
    assert len(audit_log) > 0
    assert audit_log[-1]['status'] == 'SUCCESS'

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_employee
preconditions: No active session or invalid token
actions: Attempt to view audit logs without token
expected: HTTP 401 Unauthorized
Response contains error message mentioning authentication required
No audit record created for the failed attempt
No employee data leaked in response
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_employee():
    session = requests.Session()
    emp_resp = session.get(
        'http://localhost:8080/employees/EMP-001'
    )
    assert emp_resp.status_code == 401
    resp_body = emp_resp.json()
    assert 'error' in resp_body
    assert 'authentication' in resp_body['error'].lower()
    assert 'employee_id' not in resp_body
    assert 'name' not in resp_body

tests.name: test_non_manager_role_cannot_view_employee
preconditions: User is authenticated with role=employee (not manager)
actions: GET /employees/EMP-001 with valid but insufficient token
expected: HTTP 403 Forbidden
Response contains error message mentioning role/permission denied
No employee data in response body
No audit record created for unauthorized view attempt
trace: R-WEB-004: error pathway 403 if role forbidden
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_non_manager_role_cannot_view_employee():
    session = requests.Session()
    auth_resp = session.post(
        'http://localhost:8080/auth/login',
        json={'username': 'employee_user', 'password': '<pwd_fixture>'}
    )
    assert auth_resp.status_code == 200
    token = auth_resp.json()['token']
    
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}'}
    emp_resp = session.get(
        'http://localhost:8080/employees/EMP-001',
        headers=headers
    )
    assert emp_resp.status_code == 403
    resp_body = emp_resp.json()
    assert 'error' in resp_body
    assert 'permission' in resp_body['error'].lower() or 'forbidden' in resp_body['error'].lower()
    assert 'employee_id' not in resp_body

tests.name: test_manager_views_nonexistent_employee
preconditions: Employee with id=EMP-INVALID does not exist
expected: HTTP 404 Not Found
Response contains error message mentioning employee not found
No employee data in response
Audit record may be created with status=NOT_FOUND or no record created
trace: R-WEB-004: validation error on missing resource
R-WEB-004: error pathway on invalid entity
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_manager_views_nonexistent_employee():
    session = requests.Session()
    auth_resp = session.post(
        'http://localhost:8080/auth/login',
        json={'username': 'manager_user', 'password': '<pwd_fixture>'}
    )
    assert auth_resp.status_code == 200
    token = auth_resp.json()['token']
    
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}'}
    emp_resp = session.get(
        'http://localhost:8080/employees/EMP-INVALID',
        headers=headers
    )
    assert emp_resp.status_code == 404
    resp_body = emp_resp.json()
    assert 'error' in resp_body
    assert 'not found' in resp_body['error'].lower()

tests.name: test_manager_views_employee_with_minimal_data
preconditions: Employee record exists with only mandatory fields (id, name, email)
Employee has no department, phone, or optional attributes
expected: HTTP 200 response
Response body contains all mandatory fields: employee_id, name, email
Optional fields may be null, empty string, or omitted
Audit record created successfully
Response is valid JSON with no structural errors
trace: R-WEB-004: mandatory fields must be validated
R-WEB-004: employee is viewed with minimal data
R-WEB-004: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import requests
import json

def test_manager_views_employee_with_minimal_data():
    session = requests.Session()
    auth_resp = session.post(
        'http://localhost:8080/auth/login',
        json={'username': 'manager_user', 'password': '<pwd_fixture>'}
    )
    assert auth_resp.status_code == 200
    token = auth_resp.json()['token']
    
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}'}
    emp_resp = session.get(
        'http://localhost:8080/employees/EMP-MIN',
        headers=headers
    )
    assert emp_resp.status_code == 200
    emp_data = emp_resp.json()
    assert 'employee_id' in emp_data and emp_data['employee_id'] == 'EMP-MIN'
    assert 'name' in emp_data and emp_data['name'].strip() != ''
    assert 'email' in emp_data and emp_data['email'].strip() != ''
    assert isinstance(emp_data, dict)

tests.name: test_manager_views_employee_with_special_characters
preconditions: Employee record exists with special characters in name and email fields
Employee id=EMP-SPECIAL contains name with unicode and email with special chars
expected: HTTP 200 response
Response body correctly decoded with special characters preserved
employee_id, name, email present and valid
No encoding errors or corrupted data in response
Audit record created successfully
trace: R-WEB-004: mandatory fields must be validated
R-WEB-004: employee is viewed with special characters
R-WEB-004: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import requests
import json

def test_manager_views_employee_with_special_characters():
    session = requests.Session()
    auth_resp = session.post(
        'http://localhost:8080/auth/login',
        json={'username': 'manager_user', 'password': '<pwd_fixture>'}
    )
    assert auth_resp.status_code == 200
    token = auth_resp.json()['token']
    
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}'}
    emp_resp = session.get(
        'http://localhost:8080/employees/EMP-SPECIAL',
        headers=headers
    )
    assert emp_resp.status_code == 200
    emp_data = emp_resp.json()
    assert 'employee_id' in emp_data
    assert 'name' in emp_data
    assert 'email' in emp_data
    assert isinstance(emp_data['name'], str)
    assert isinstance(emp_data['email'], str)
